sections.paragraph:
μείωσις, εως, ἡ (s. μειόω; Hippocr. et al.; Polyb. 9, 43, 5; Sext. Emp., Math. 9, 400; Vett. Val. index; PMich XIII, 659, 227 [VI a.d.] taxes; Philo) lessening, diminution τῆς σαρκός mutilation of the flesh Dg 4:4.—DELG s.v. μείων. New Docs 2, 78.

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Sext. Emp. Sext. Emp. = Sextus Empiricus, II–III a.d.—List 5

Math Math = mathematician

PMich PMich = Michigan Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 626
